title: Google STEP Internship — Summer 2026
deadline: Rolling — apply early for summer cohorts
eligibility: First- and second-year undergraduates in CS or related fields; US work authorization required
whyItMatters: STEP is Google's dedicated pipeline for early undergrads — one of the few top-tier internships explicitly designed for freshmen and sophomores.
goodFor: First- or second-year CS students who want a structured intro to big-tech SWE before junior recruiting.
nextAction: Create a Google Careers profile, upload resume, and apply to STEP roles as they post for your graduation year.

title: Microsoft Explore Program — Summer 2026
deadline: Rolling — fall recruiting for following summer
location: Redmond, WA and other US sites
eligibility: Freshmen and sophomores in CS, CE, or related majors; US students
whyItMatters: Explore gives early students a 12-week rotational SWE experience and a strong signal for returning as a SWE intern.
goodFor: Underclassmen who want exposure to multiple Microsoft teams before committing to a specialty.
nextAction: Search 'Explore' on Microsoft Careers Students portal and submit one tailored application per open requisition.

title: Meta University Engineering — Summer 2026
location: Menlo Park, CA; Seattle, WA; New York, NY
eligibility: Rising juniors and seniors from underrepresented backgrounds in tech; US work authorization
whyItMatters: Meta University Engineering is a dedicated intern track with mentorship for students from groups historically underrepresented in tech.
goodFor: Eligible rising juniors/seniors seeking a structured Meta internship with cohort support.
nextAction: Check metacareers.com/students for Meta University Engineering openings and apply when your cohort is listed.

title: NVIDIA AI / Deep Learning Intern — Summer 2026
location: Santa Clara, CA; Austin, TX; remote-eligible roles vary
eligibility: Junior or senior undergraduates or MS students with ML coursework and Python/C++ experience
whyItMatters: NVIDIA sits at the center of the AI hardware stack — interning here signals serious ML systems credibility.
goodFor: Students with PyTorch/TensorFlow projects who want GPU-scale AI engineering experience.
nextAction: Filter Workday listings for 'Intern' + 'Deep Learning' or 'AI' and apply with a project-heavy resume.

title: Amazon SDE Intern — Summer 2026
location: Seattle, WA; Austin, TX; multiple US hubs
eligibility: Bachelor's students graduating after internship term; CS or related major preferred
whyItMatters: Amazon hires thousands of SDE interns annually — high-upside if you can clear their bar-raiser loop.
goodFor: Students with solid DSA fundamentals ready for OA + multiple behavioral/technical interviews.
nextAction: Apply via amazon.jobs for 'Software Development Engineer Intern' matching your grad year and preferred location.

title: JPMorgan Chase Software Engineer Summer Intern — 2026
location: New York, NY; Columbus, OH; Plano, TX; other US offices
eligibility: Undergraduates in CS, CE, or related fields; juniors most common
whyItMatters: JPMorgan's SWE intern program offers strong return-offer rates and production-scale fintech engineering.
goodFor: Students interested in distributed systems, trading tech, or enterprise Java/Kotlin stacks.
nextAction: Apply on JPMorgan's student programs portal for Software Engineer Summer Analyst roles.

title: Stripe Software Engineering Intern — Summer 2026
location: San Francisco, CA; Seattle, WA; remote-hybrid varies
eligibility: Undergraduate and graduate students with strong programming fundamentals
whyItMatters: Stripe interns ship real payment infrastructure — strong brand for backend-minded engineers.
goodFor: Students who enjoy API design, reliability engineering, and writing careful production code.
nextAction: Filter stripe.com/jobs for intern roles and apply with a resume highlighting backend or full-stack projects.

title: GitHub Octernships — 2026 Cohort
deadline: Cohort-based — check site for next window
eligibility: Students and early-career developers; often targets underrepresented groups in open source
whyItMatters: Octernships pair students with open-source maintainers — unique path for OSS-minded engineers.
goodFor: Students with GitHub contributions who want remote, mission-driven internship experience.
nextAction: Watch github.com/industry for the next Octernships application window and prepare a contribution portfolio.

title: Figma Software Engineer Intern — Summer 2026
location: San Francisco, CA; New York, NY
eligibility: Undergraduate or graduate students in CS or related fields
whyItMatters: Figma blends product design and systems programming — strong signal for frontend-leaning engineers.
goodFor: Students with TypeScript/React experience and interest in collaborative creative tools.
nextAction: Search Figma careers for 'Intern' roles and apply with a portfolio link showing UI or graphics work.

title: Databricks Software Engineering Intern — Summer 2026
eligibility: CS or related undergraduates; Spark or distributed systems exposure a plus
whyItMatters: Databricks interns work on production data infrastructure used by thousands of enterprises.
goodFor: Students with data engineering or backend projects who want scale-up experience before IPO-era companies.
nextAction: Filter Databricks open positions for university intern roles and apply with a data-project highlight.

title: Scale AI Machine Learning Intern — Summer 2026
eligibility: Strong ML fundamentals; undergraduates and graduate students
whyItMatters: Scale sits at the intersection of AI training data and production ML pipelines.
goodFor: Students targeting applied ML roles who want startup velocity and AI infrastructure exposure.
nextAction: Browse scale.com/careers for intern listings and tailor your resume to ML projects and Python proficiency.

title: Rippling Software Engineer Intern — Summer 2026
eligibility: CS undergraduates; full-stack or backend experience preferred
whyItMatters: Rippling moves fast on HR/payroll infrastructure — good for generalist engineers who like ownership.
goodFor: Students who want startup intern intensity with a clearer path than pre-seed companies.
nextAction: Search Rippling careers for Software Engineer Intern and submit with a full-stack project demo link.

title: TAMU Student Worker — Dining Services
location: College Station, TX — campus dining locations
eligibility: Enrolled Texas A&M students with work-study or student employment eligibility
whyItMatters: On-campus dining jobs are one of the fastest ways for TAMU students to earn steady part-time income near classes.
goodFor: Aggies who need flexible hours, minimal commute, and a low-barrier first job on campus.
nextAction: Log into Jobs for Aggies, search Dining Services openings, and apply to a location near your schedule.

title: TAMU Rec Sports — Lifeguard / Aquatics Staff
deadline: Rolling — peak hiring before fall and summer sessions
location: College Station, TX — Student Rec Center
eligibility: TAMU students; lifeguard certification required or provided via training
whyItMatters: Rec Sports hires hundreds of students yearly — aquatics roles pay above minimum and build leadership experience.
goodFor: Active students willing to get lifeguard certified who want structured campus employment.
nextAction: Search 'Rec Sports' or 'Aquatics' on Jobs for Aggies and note certification requirements in your application.

title: TAMU Evans Library — Student Library Assistant
eligibility: Enrolled TAMU students; federal work-study preferred for some shifts
whyItMatters: Library assistant roles offer calm work environments and schedules that align with academic calendars.
goodFor: Students who want low-stress campus work with predictable shifts between classes.
nextAction: Filter Jobs for Aggies by 'University Libraries' and apply to Evans or branch library openings.

title: UT Austin CNS IT — Student Technology Assistant
organization: UT Austin College of Natural Sciences
eligibility: UT Austin students in CNS or with IT support experience
whyItMatters: CNS IT student roles build help-desk and systems skills while paying on campus.
goodFor: UT CS or natural sciences students who want résumé-relevant campus tech work.
nextAction: Log into HireUTexas and search CNS IT student technician postings.

title: Chegg Expert Tutor — Computer Science
eligibility: Undergraduate or graduate students with strong grades in CS coursework; subject test required
whyItMatters: Chegg Expert lets strong CS students monetize coursework knowledge on a flexible schedule.
goodFor: Students who aced data structures, calculus, or programming courses and want remote income.
nextAction: Apply at chegg.com/tutors, pass the subject screening test, and set availability for evenings/weekends.

title: Rev — Freelance Transcriptionist Application
eligibility: US residents 18+; pass grammar and typing assessment
whyItMatters: Rev offers immediate remote income without requiring technical skills — useful between semesters.
goodFor: Students who need fast cash and have strong typing accuracy and attention to detail.
nextAction: Complete Rev's freelancer application and grammar quiz; start with short transcription jobs to build rating.

title: UserTesting — Paid Website & App Studies
eligibility: 18+ with microphone and stable internet; practice test required
whyItMatters: Low-effort paid studies add up quickly and expose you to how real products get user feedback.
goodFor: Students with 15–30 spare minutes who want pocket money without a formal job commitment.
nextAction: Sign up, pass the practice test, and enable notifications for new study invitations.

title: Tutor.com — Online Computer Science Tutor
eligibility: College students with B+ or better in tutored subjects; background check required
whyItMatters: Tutor.com contracts with libraries and schools — steady demand for CS homework help.
goodFor: Strong students who enjoy explaining algorithms, Java, Python, or discrete math.
nextAction: Apply at tutor.com/apply, select Computer Science subjects, and complete the subject proficiency exam.

title: NextGenHacks 2026
eligibility: Students above legal age of majority; solo or team
whyItMatters: Beginner-friendly online hackathon with a real deadline — strong first portfolio project opportunity.
goodFor: Students new to hackathons who want a low-pressure, open-ended build weekend.
nextAction: Register on nextgenhacks.devpost.com and submit project title, description, and demo by the deadline.

title: HackUTD 2025
deadline: Event concluded — watch hackutd.co for HackUTD 2026
eligibility: University students; check hackutd.co for next event rules
whyItMatters: HackUTD is one of the largest university hackathons in Texas — major recruiting presence from sponsors.
goodFor: Texas CS students who want in-person hackathon experience and sponsor networking.
nextAction: Follow hackutd.co for HackUTD 2026 registration; review 2025 Devpost gallery for project inspiration.

title: GitLab AI Hackathon 2026 — Duo Agent Platform
eligibility: Global developers; built agents on GitLab Duo Agent Platform
whyItMatters: This hackathon focused on building real AI agents on GitLab Duo — a reference for agentic DevOps portfolios.
goodFor: Students interested in AI agents and CI/CD who want to study winning project patterns.
nextAction: Browse winners at gitlab.devpost.com/project-gallery; sign up at contributors.gitlab.com for the next event.

title: Google Cloud Rapid Agent Hackathon 2026
eligibility: Builders using Gemini + Google Cloud Agent Builder with partner MCP integration
whyItMatters: Premier agentic-AI hackathon with $60K prizes across MongoDB, Elastic, GitLab, and other MCP tracks.
goodFor: Students who built or want to study production-style AI agents with Google Cloud tooling.
nextAction: Review submitted projects on rapid-agent.devpost.com; apply lessons to your next agent portfolio piece.

title: TreeHacks 2026
compensation: Travel reimbursements may be available for accepted hackers
eligibility: University students worldwide; application required
whyItMatters: TreeHacks is a top-tier West Coast hackathon with heavy Big Tech and startup sponsor presence.
goodFor: Strong builders seeking a competitive hackathon with high recruiting visibility.
nextAction: Monitor treehacks.com for the 2026 hacker application and prepare a one-page project portfolio.

title: CodePath TIP — Fall 2026 Cohort
eligibility: US university students in CS or related fields; GitHub account required
whyItMatters: CodePath TIP is the most proven free interview-prep pipeline for students targeting top SWE internships.
goodFor: CS students who need structured DSA practice before junior-year recruiting season.
nextAction: Create a CodePath account at applications.codepath.org and complete the TIP application plus HackerRank assessment.

title: CodePath Intro to Cybersecurity — Fall 2026
eligibility: US university students; introductory security course with prework assessment
whyItMatters: 10-week cohort gives hands-on security fundamentals with industry mentors — rare free structured path into cyber.
goodFor: Students curious about security careers who want guided labs beyond CTFs.
nextAction: Apply via applications.codepath.org, complete level prework, and block 8–10 hrs/week for the cohort.

title: Headstarter Residency
deadline: Rolling — new cohorts start every 2 weeks
eligibility: Aspiring SWE/MLE builders; portfolio-oriented application
whyItMatters: Residency members ship 8–26 production-style projects with 1:1 mentorship — strong for weak-resume students.
goodFor: Builders who need depth over breadth in AI/full-stack portfolio projects before recruiting.
nextAction: Submit the short application at apply.theheadstarter.com with links to your best 1–2 projects.

title: Headstarter Accelerator
eligibility: Students and early-career engineers interested in AI/ML and full-stack builds
whyItMatters: Shorter commitment than Residency but still includes mock interviews and mentor intros.
goodFor: Students who want structured project sprints plus interview practice in one program.
nextAction: Apply at accelerator.headstarter.co and join the next cohort kickoff session.

title: Neo Scholars — 2025 Cycle
deadline: 2025 cycle closed — join interest list for 2026
compensation: Mentorship, recruiting platform access, founder network
eligibility: Entrepreneurial CS undergraduates at US universities
whyItMatters: Neo Scholars connects top entrepreneurial undergrads with founders and a curated recruiting platform.
goodFor: Startup-minded CS students planning ahead for the next Neo cycle.
nextAction: Join the interest list at neo.com/apply and follow Neo newsletters for 2026 application dates.

title: MLH Fellowship — Open Source Track
eligibility: Students and recent grads; strong GitHub and open-source interest
whyItMatters: MLH Fellowship is a paid remote program contributing to major open-source projects.
goodFor: Students with existing OSS contributions who want a stipend-backed summer alternative to traditional internships.
nextAction: Review fellowship.mlh.io tracks, polish your GitHub profile, and apply when the next cohort opens.

title: ColorStack Membership Application
eligibility: Black and Latinx university students in computing majors
whyItMatters: ColorStack membership unlocks referrals, interview prep, and a national community for Black and Latinx CS students.
goodFor: Eligible computing majors who want peer support and recruiter access beyond solo applications.
nextAction: Complete the member application at app.colorstack.io/apply and join your campus chapter.

title: Coca-Cola Scholars Program — Class of 2027
eligibility: US high school seniors with min 3.0 GPA; leadership and service required
whyItMatters: One of the largest merit-based scholarships for student leaders — $20K with national recognition.
goodFor: High-achieving high school seniors with strong extracurricular leadership records.
nextAction: Review eligibility at coca-colascholarsfoundation.org/apply and start essays early.

title: The Gates Scholarship — Class of 2027
eligibility: Pell-eligible high school seniors from underrepresented backgrounds; min 3.3 GPA
whyItMatters: The Gates Scholarship covers full cost of attendance for exceptional Pell-eligible students.
goodFor: High-achieving seniors from low-income backgrounds planning four-year college.
nextAction: Confirm Pell eligibility and begin the application at thegatesscholarship.org.

title: Dell Scholars Program — 2027
eligibility: US students in an approved college readiness program; demonstrated need and grit
whyItMatters: Dell Scholars provides financial aid plus ongoing coaching — not just a one-time check.
goodFor: Students overcoming significant obstacles who are in college-readiness programs.
nextAction: Check dellscholars.org eligibility and gather recommendation letters before December deadline.

title: Generation Google Scholarship — 2026–2027
deadline: Varies by region — US window typically spring
eligibility: Underrepresented students in computer science; strong academic record
whyItMatters: Generation Google Scholarship combines financial aid with Google community access for underrepresented CS students.
goodFor: Eligible CS undergraduates who want scholarship funding plus Google network exposure.
nextAction: Monitor buildyourfuture.withgoogle.com for the US application window and prepare resume plus essays.

title: NSF REU — CISE Sites (Computer & Information Science)
deadline: Varies by site — typically January–March for summer
eligibility: US citizens/permanent residents; undergraduates with relevant coursework
whyItMatters: NSF REU sites are the gold standard for paid undergraduate research — essential for PhD-bound students.
goodFor: Sophomores and juniors targeting graduate school or research-heavy industry labs.
nextAction: Search NSF REU CISE sites, pick 3–5 labs aligned with your interests, and apply to each directly.

title: Ai2 Research Engineering Internship
eligibility: Undergraduate and graduate students with ML/NLP research experience
whyItMatters: Ai2 publishes at top venues — interning here is a direct path to competitive AI research careers.
goodFor: Students with prior research or strong ML projects who want nonprofit AI lab experience.
nextAction: Browse allenai.org/careers for intern postings and email teams with relevant paper implementations.

title: MIT Lincoln Laboratory — Summer Research Program
eligibility: US citizens; undergraduate and graduate students in STEM
whyItMatters: Lincoln Lab offers cutting-edge applied research in radar, cyber, and AI for national security.
goodFor: US-citizen STEM students interested in applied R&D outside academia.
nextAction: Review ll.mit.edu student opportunities and apply during the fall recruiting window.

title: NSF REU — UT Arlington CSE Research Site (Summer 2026)
eligibility: US citizen/permanent resident undergraduates; CS/CSE preferred
whyItMatters: Example of a specific NSF REU site — Texas students can target local CISE REU programs each summer.
goodFor: Texas undergraduates who want paid summer research without leaving the state.
nextAction: Bookmark UTA CSE REU for next cycle; search NSF REU directory for other Texas sites open now.

title: CMU Robotics Institute — Summer Scholars
eligibility: Undergraduate students nationwide; strong STEM background
whyItMatters: CMU Robotics Summer Scholars is among the most competitive undergrad robotics research programs.
goodFor: Students passionate about robotics, perception, or autonomous systems research.
nextAction: Prepare for next January's application with a robotics project portfolio and faculty reference.

title: TAMU HireAggies — Fall 2026 Engineering Internship & Career Fair
eligibility: Texas A&M students and alumni; resume required for some employers
whyItMatters: The fall engineering fair is TAMU's highest-density recruiting event for internships and co-ops.
goodFor: Aggie engineering and CS students targeting on-campus recruiter conversations.
nextAction: Register on HireAggies Career Hub, print 20+ resumes, and research attending employers beforehand.

title: TAMU Computer Science — Department Ambassadors
deadline: Applications open each spring for following academic year
eligibility: TAMU CS undergraduates with strong communication skills and 3.0+ GPA
whyItMatters: Department ambassadors lead tours and represent CS — visible leadership for recruiting conversations.
goodFor: TAMU CS students who want low-time-commitment campus leadership on their resume.
nextAction: Watch for spring ambassador applications on the CSE undergraduate page.

title: TAMU Memorial Student Center — Student Committee Application
eligibility: Enrolled TAMU students committed to MSC programming
whyItMatters: MSC committees run major campus events — strong soft-skill signal for behavioral interviews.
goodFor: Aggies who want structured leadership outside Greek life or student government.
nextAction: Attend MSC open house in August and submit a committee preference application.

title: UT Austin Engineering Expo — Spring 2026
organization: UT Austin Cockrell School of Engineering
deadline: Event-day check-in — register on Handshake beforehand
eligibility: UT Austin engineering and CS students
whyItMatters: Engineering Expo concentrates hundreds of employers for UT students in one day.
goodFor: UT CS and engineering students preparing for internship recruiting season.
nextAction: RSVP on Handshake, download the employer list, and prepare 30-second pitches for top targets.

title: Break Through Tech AI Fellowship
location: Multiple US campus hubs + online components
eligibility: Women and underrepresented students in computing at partner universities
whyItMatters: Break Through Tech AI Fellowship bridges coursework to industry AI roles with structured skill-building.
goodFor: Eligible women and non-binary students at partner schools pursuing AI careers.
nextAction: Confirm your school is a Break Through Tech partner and apply when the AI Fellowship window opens.

title: Microsoft LEAP Apprenticeship Program
location: Redmond, WA and other Microsoft hubs
eligibility: Non-traditional candidates including career changers and bootcamp grads; varies by cohort
whyItMatters: LEAP is a paid apprenticeship pathway into Microsoft for candidates without traditional CS degrees.
goodFor: Late-stage students or recent grads from non-traditional backgrounds targeting Microsoft SWE roles.
nextAction: Review LEAP cohort requirements at microsoft.com/leap and apply to the next regional cohort.

title: Apple Developer Academy — 2026 Cohort
deadline: Varies by academy location — US Detroit academy opens annually
eligibility: Students and entrepreneurs 18+; design and development interest required
whyItMatters: Apple Developer Academy trains iOS developers and founders in a year-long immersive program.
goodFor: Students passionate about Swift, mobile UX, and shipping App Store products.
nextAction: Review developer.apple.com/academies for Detroit cohort dates and submit the multi-step application.

title: Palantir Path Fellowship
location: New York, NY; Denver, CO; Washington, DC
eligibility: Students from underrepresented backgrounds in tech; strong problem-solving skills
whyItMatters: Palantir Path provides a structured on-ramp to Palantir engineering for underrepresented students.
goodFor: Strong analytical students interested in data platforms and mission-driven software.
nextAction: Check palantir.com/careers/students for Path Fellowship openings and submit application with project writeups.

title: Kode With Klossy — Summer 2026 Camp
deadline: March 2026 for summer camps (verify for late cohorts)
eligibility: Girls and non-binary students ages 13–18; no prior coding experience required
whyItMatters: Free intensive coding camps that launch thousands of young women into CS pathways each summer.
goodFor: High school girls and non-binary students exploring their first CS experience.
nextAction: Bookmark kodewithklossy.com for 2027 applications if you missed the 2026 window.
